name: Primary Genre
description: The core gameplay genre that best describes how the game plays.

values.name: Shooter
values.description: First or third-person shooters, including FPS, battle royale, and tactical military combat games.

values.name: Strategy & Management
values.description: 4X, grand strategy, city-builders, tycoons, transport sims, and base/empire management.

values.name: RPG
values.description: Role-playing games including action RPGs, JRPGs, MMORPGs, CRPGs, and cultivation/wuxia RPGs.

values.name: Survival & Sandbox
values.description: Open-ended crafting, survival, farming, and life simulation sandboxes.

values.name: Action & Platformer
values.description: Action games, platformers, fighting/brawlers, hack-and-slash, and roguelite action.

values.name: Adventure & Narrative
values.description: Story-driven adventures, walking sims, point-and-click, visual novels, and detective games.

values.name: Puzzle & Party
values.description: Puzzle games, party games, deck-builders, card games, and rhythm games.

values.name: Sports & Racing
values.description: Racing simulators, arcade racers, and sports simulations like soccer or basketball.

values.name: Other
values.description: None of the above values clearly fit. When picking Other, also provide a 2-5 word phrase in the corresponding _other field describing what would actually fit.

name: Multiplayer Mode
description: How the game is intended to be played socially.

values.name: Single-Player Focus
values.description: Designed primarily as a solo experience with little or no multiplayer component.

values.name: Co-op
values.description: Cooperative play where players team up against the game or AI.

values.name: Competitive PvP
values.description: Player-versus-player focus including arena, battle royale, and esports titles.

values.name: Persistent Online World
values.description: MMOs and always-online shared worlds with many concurrent players.

values.name: Local Party / Couch
values.description: Best played with friends in the same room via local or chaotic party modes.

values.name: Optional Multiplayer
values.description: Primarily single-player but with meaningful drop-in co-op or PvP modes.

values.name: Other
values.description: None of the above values clearly fit. When picking Other, also provide a 2-5 word phrase in the corresponding _other field describing what would actually fit.

name: Setting & Aesthetic
description: The fictional world, era, and visual style the game evokes.

values.name: High Fantasy
values.description: Swords, magic, dragons, and medieval-fantasy worlds.

values.name: Sci-Fi & Space
values.description: Futuristic, space-faring, cyberpunk, or alien-world settings.

values.name: Modern & Contemporary
values.description: Present-day real-world settings including urban, suburban, or rural.

values.name: Historical
values.description: Grounded in real historical eras like WWII, antiquity, feudal Asia, or noir periods.

values.name: Post-Apocalyptic & Horror
values.description: Wastelands, zombie outbreaks, haunted spaces, and dark/dystopian worlds.

values.name: Anime & Stylized
values.description: Anime, manga, or highly stylized cartoon aesthetics including visual novels.

values.name: Cozy & Whimsical
values.description: Charming, pastoral, or cute worlds with warm, low-stakes vibes.

values.name: Other
values.description: None of the above values clearly fit. When picking Other, also provide a 2-5 word phrase in the corresponding _other field describing what would actually fit.

name: Pacing & Tone
description: The emotional and rhythmic feel of moment-to-moment play.

values.name: Adrenaline & Twitch
values.description: Fast reflexes, high-intensity combat, and split-second decisions.

values.name: Tense & Scary
values.description: Horror, suspense, and creeping dread as the dominant mood.

values.name: Cerebral & Methodical
values.description: Slow, thoughtful play emphasizing planning, puzzle-solving, or strategic depth.

values.name: Epic & Cinematic
values.description: Big sweeping narratives, dramatic set-pieces, and heroic adventures.

values.name: Chaotic & Comedic
values.description: Slapstick, absurdist, or goofy gameplay built around laughter and mayhem.

values.name: Relaxing & Cozy
values.description: Low-pressure, meditative experiences meant to soothe rather than challenge.

values.name: Grindy & Progression-Driven
values.description: Long-haul loops centered on leveling, looting, or incremental mastery.

values.name: Other
values.description: None of the above values clearly fit. When picking Other, also provide a 2-5 word phrase in the corresponding _other field describing what would actually fit.

name: Production Scale
description: The apparent budget and scope of the title.

values.name: AAA Blockbuster
values.description: Big-budget productions from major publishers with high fidelity and broad scope.

values.name: Mid-Tier Studio
values.description: Established studios making polished games at a smaller scope than AAA.

values.name: Indie
values.description: Small-team independent productions with distinctive creative voices.

values.name: Niche Simulator
values.description: Focused, often unglamorous simulators serving a dedicated hobbyist audience.

values.name: Free-to-Play Live Service
values.description: Free-to-download games sustained by ongoing updates, seasons, and microtransactions.

values.name: Tool / Utility
values.description: Software utilities, creation tools, or training apps rather than traditional games.

values.name: Other
values.description: None of the above values clearly fit. When picking Other, also provide a 2-5 word phrase in the corresponding _other field describing what would actually fit.
